recipe.title: Homestyle Rustic Indian Chicken Curry

ingredients.item: regular Indian red chili powder, or to taste
(454 g) boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into about 1½-inch cubes (see Variations)
medium tomatoes, puréed or finely chopped
to 10 sprigs fresh cilantro, leaves roughly chopped, for garnishing

steps: Combine all the ingredients for the marinade in a medium bowl and toss well to ensure the chicken pieces are well and evenly coated.
Cover with a lid or plastic wrap and let is sit on the counter for 30 minutes or so while you prepare the curry (see FAQs & Notes for more information).
In a medium pot or a deep sauté pan, heat oil over medium heat and add the oil. When the oil starts shimmering add in the cumin and mustard seeds along with asafetida.
When the seeds sizzle and the asafetida froths, add the onion. Season the onion with salt to help it soften. Sauté for 4-5 minutes, stirring it frequently, until it is translucent.
Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and sauté for 1 minute.
Stir in the turmeric and garam masala. Sauté the mixture for about 30 seconds to let the spices bloom.
Add the puréed or chopped tomatoes. Season the mixture with additional salt and stir the ingredients to combine them well.
Cook uncovered for 5-7 minutes on medium-low heat till most of the moisture is absorbed and you can see tiny drops of oil floating on the surface.
Toss in the marinated chicken and mix well to combine.
Simmer covered for 10-12 minutes, till the chicken is fully cooked.
Continue simmering uncovered for additional 8-10 minutes to let the excess moisture absorb and evaporate, while stirring occasionally.
Garnish with cilantro. Serve immediately (see FAQs & Notes for storing leftovers or meal prepping information).
Place the inner pot in the Instant Pot and press the Sauté button.
Add the oil along with cumin and mustard seeds along with asafetida.
When the seeds splutter, add the onions. Sprinkle some salt and sauté till the onion turns light brown in color, for about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the ginger- garlic paste and sauté the mixture for 1 minute.
Add the turmeric and garam masala and sauté the mixture for 30 seconds to let the spices bloom.

Mix in the tomatoes and stir to combine the ingredients. Deglaze the bottom of the pot by scraping it with a wooden spoon. This is an important step; if the bottom of the pot is not deglazed well, the “burn” error might appear during the pressure-cooking stage.
Cook the mixture for 5 minutes, until the tomatoes lose most of their moisture.
Press the Cancel button to turn off the Instant Pot.
Add the marinated chicken along with 1/4 cup of water.
Toss the chicken with the onion-tomato mixture and stir to combine the ingredients.
Close the lid and turn the steam release valve to the sealing position. Press the Poultry/ Manual/ Pressure Cook button and set the time for 10 minutes on high pressure.
When cooking is complete, open the lid after the pressure has released naturally. It will take about 15 -20 minutes to depressurize.

Using a slotted spoon, transfer the cooked chicken to a clean medium bowl. Press the Sauté button and bring the sauce to a boil. Stir the curry continuously for 5 to 7 minutes, until most of the moisture is evaporated and the oil has separated from the sauce. Transfer the chicken back to the reduced curry and toss to combine it well with the curry. Press the Cancel button to turn off the Instant Pot.
Garnish with cilantro/coriander and serve hot with rotis or rice.

recipe.title: Mediterranean Olive Oil Pasta

ingredients.item: Early Harvest Greek Extra Virgin Olive Oil
scallions, (green onions, top trimmed, both whites and greens chopped)
crumbled feta cheese, (more if you like)

steps: Cook spaghetti: Follow package instructions to cook the spaghetti to al dente in plenty of boiling water with salt and olive oil. For me, it took about 6 minutes.

Make the sauce: When the pasta is almost cooked, set a large skillet over medium heat, and add extra virgin olive oil. Once the oil begins to shimmer, add garlic and a pinch of salt. Cook for 10 seconds, stirring regularly. Reduce the heat to low, stir in the parsley, tomatoes, and chopped scallions. Cook until just warmed through, about 30 to 60 seconds.
Drain the pasta: When the pasta is ready, remove from heat, drain the spaghetti, and add it to the skillet with the sauce. Toss to coat thoroughly. Add black pepper and toss again to coat.

Finish and serve: Add the artichokes, olives, feta, basil, lemon zest, and crushed red pepper flakes to taste (if using). Toss one more time. Serve immediately in pasta bowls, and if you like, top each with more basil leaves and feta. Enjoy!

recipe.title: Indian Ginger Garlic Chicken
steps: Cut chicken breast into 1-inch pieces and place in a large bowl. In a food processor, combine ginger, 4 garlic cloves, and chili and pulsate to make a paste. Mix the paste with yogurt. Stir in the salt and lemon juice. Add the mixture to the chicken and marinate for a couple of hours.
To a large cast-iron or nonstick skillet, add olive oil, smashed 2 garlic cloves, cloves, and cardamom. Heat the skillet over medium-low heat.

When the oil is hot enough and garlic and spices are sizzled through, remove them from the skillet (reserving the oil). Add onions and sauté until translucent, about 2 minutes. Add marinated chicken and turmeric (for intense yellow color).
Cook on medium-low heat stirring occasionally for 20-30 minutes or until juices run clear.

recipe.title: Vegetarian Mediterranean Pasta with Feta and Artichokes

ingredients.item: kosher salt and black pepper, (to taste)

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the spaghetti according to the package directions.
Scoop out and reserve 1 cup of the pasta water. Drain the spaghetti.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of the olive oil on medium high until shimmering.
Add the sliced bell peppers and saute until they are a little browned around the edges, about 5 minutes.

Turn the heat down to low and add the artichokes, olives, parsley, garlic, scallions, and red pepper flakes along with the rest of the olive oil. Saute for 2-3 minutes. Set the sauce aside until the pasta is done.
Once it ia cooked, add the drained pasta and 1/2 cup of the water to the skillet with the sauce.
Toss and simmer together until the sauce thickens and clings to the pasta. If you don't have enough liquid, add more of the water.
Stir in the fresh lemon juice.
Taste and add salt and/or pepper as needed.
Serve topped with the crumbled feta.

recipe.title: Dahi Chicken Recipe (Indian Yogurt Chicken)

ingredients.item: Kashmiri Red Chili Powder - adjust to taste (skip if not available, add regular chili powder)
Kasoori Methi - optional but adds an amazing aromatic flavor
Chicken - preferably boneless thighs or drumsticks
Light Oil - can use olive oil or mustard oil works best
Butter - can be salted or plain (adjust salt accordingly)
Ginger + Garlic - grated together or use paste

steps: Heat oil in a large pan or skillet over medium heat.
Add cumin seeds, cinnamon stick, bay leaf, and crushed green cardamom and let them splutter.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown. This step enhances the sweetness of the onions.
Add ginger and garlic, and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the washed and cleaned chicken to the pan and sear on all sides.
Add turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, and salt. Saute everything.
Cover and let the chicken cook on medium-low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Once the chicken is 90% cooked (you can poke the piece with a spatula and the skin should come off easily), add the whisked yogurt.
Stir continuously while adding the yogurt to prevent curdling.
Simmer for 5-7 minutes until the gravy thickens and coats the chicken beautifully.
Sprinkle garam masala, and kasoori methi and stir everything together for another 2-3 minutes.
Do the quick salt and spices taste test and then turn off the heat.
Garnish with fresh cilantro.
Serve hot with steamed rice, naan, or roti or choice or grains or breads.

recipe.title: Mediterranean Pasta with Feta and Olives

ingredients.item: x 400 g (14 oz) can chopped tomatoes
(3.5 oz) Kalamata olives, pitted and roughly chopped
(3.5 oz) feta cheese, cut int bite-size pieces
Freshly ground black pepper to taste

steps: Bring a large pot of water to a boil, salt it generously and cook the pasta al dente according to the instructions on the package. Reserve 1-2 cups of starchy pasta water before draining.
Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a large pan and saute the red onion for 2-3 minutes over medium heat.
When the onion has softened a bit, stir in the garlic and continue to cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the tomato paste, dried oregano, dried basil and red chilli flakes, then add the chopped tomatoes.
Allow the sauce to simmer for a minute, then stir in the fresh parsley, Kalamata olives and artichokes.
Add a dash of pasta water if the sauce looks too thick, and then stir in the feta cheese. Add the cooked linguine to the pan and toss gently until they’re well covered in sauce.
Sprinkle the fresh basil on top, season with freshly ground black pepper, divide into bowls and serve immediately.

recipe.title: Mediterranean Roasted Vegetable Pasta

ingredients.item: zucchini ((medium size) sliced lengthwise then cut into a large dice)
red bell pepper (cut in large dice)
farfalle ((or other short pasta such as penne))

steps: Preheat the oven the 425°F.

Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Place the zucchini, bell pepper, tomatoes, onion, and garlic on the lined pan. Add the olive oil and salt and pepper, to taste. Toss the vegetables with your hands so that they are evenly coated. Spread out in a single layer.
Roast the vegetables in the oven for 30 minutes until lightly browned, stirring after about 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, boil the water for the pasta. Cook the pasta according the the package directions, reserving about ½ cup of the starchy cooking water before draining.
Return the cooked pasta to the pot.
When the vegetables are done roasting, add them along with any liquid on the baking sheet to the pasta.
Toss the vegetables with the pasta, adding any of the reserved cooking water as needed to make the vegetables stick to the noodles.
Garnish with torn fresh basil leaves and serve.
